document.title: Software Validation Report for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices, May 11, 2005
Requirements for Cybersecurity of Medical Devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software validation report for AXD software used with G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports technical documentation by describing software requirements, design, verification, validation, traceability, hazard analysis, cybersecurity controls, versioning and unresolved anomalies.
keyPoints: AXD performs image acquisition, image correction and pre-processing for the detector.
The report concludes a Moderate level of concern applies to the AXD software.
Module verification and system validation tables report passing results for common functions, image acquisition, calibration, firmware update, iMX8, cybersecurity and error control functions.
Hazard analysis lists risk controls for image transmission, X-ray exposure energy, firmware, shock, parameters, Wi-Fi communication, sensors, login and data encryption.
Unresolved anomalies are listed as None.

evidence.claim: The document is Software Validation Report A-TCF-10-131 Rev.0 for the G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Validation Report for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Report No.: A-TCF-10-131 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The document revision date is stated as Nov/23/2024 in the document header.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-TCF-10-131 Rev. No. 0 Software Validation Report Rev. Date Nov/23/2024

evidence.claim: The applicable detector models are G1417CW, G1717CW, G1417FW, G1717FW, G1417CWP, G1717CWP, G1417FWP and G1717FWP.
evidence.evidenceText: The Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector can be selected between G1417CW, G1717CW, G1417FW, G1717FW, G1417CWP, G1717CWP, G1417FWP and G1717FWP.
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: Performance and usage are the same except for size, with no difference in software validation.
evidence.evidenceText: Performance and usage are all the same except for size, so there is no difference in 'Software Validation' (A-TCF-10-131)
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: AXD performs image acquisition, image correction and pre-processing.
evidence.evidenceText: AXD is software of Detector that performs image acquisition, image correction, and pre-processing.

evidence.claim: The report concludes that AXD software has a Moderate level of concern.
evidence.evidenceText: According to below responds, we conclude a Moderate level of concern applies to the AXD software. Level of Concern: Moderate

evidence.claim: The level of concern section uses FDA software premarket submission guidance dated May 11, 2005.
evidence.evidenceText: The following section includes the information required in the FDA guidance document entitled “Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices”, May 11, 2005.

evidence.claim: The software development process claims fulfillment of 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.
evidence.evidenceText: The development process fulfils the requirements 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.

evidence.claim: The software includes common functions, image acquisition, calibration, firmware update and iMX8 modules.
evidence.evidenceText: Common functions module: Control detector setup; Image acquisition module: Control image read; Calibration module: Calibration setup and pre-pre-processing control; Firmware update: Write detector firmware update file and read information; iMX8 Module: System initialization and task creation.

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity requirements include Wi-Fi login password control and authentication/encryption.
evidence.evidenceText: SRS-C-1 Wi-Fi Login Password Random access should be limited by setting ID and password when accessing the wireless network. SRS-C-2 Authentication and encryption Transmission data should be protected by applying encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, WPAPSK+TKIP, etc.

evidence.claim: Error control requirements include recovery mode for detector-SDK disconnection and calibration image support for image processing errors.
evidence.evidenceText: SRS-E-1 Connect Error It should support recovery mode to enable reconnection in case of disconnection between the detector and SDK software. SRS-E-2 Image Processing Error When noise or line is appeared during the pre-processing the image received from the detector, it should provide a calibration image for normal process.

evidence.claim: Calibration applies offset correction, gain correction and defect correction to acquired images.
evidence.evidenceText: Calibration performs following compensations to acquired images. 5.1.1. Offset Correction 5.1.2. Gain Correction 5.1.3. Defect Correction

evidence.claim: Module verification concluded the installed software on a standard PC was successful.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary: As a result of module verification, we positively evaluate the success in installed Software on standard PC.

evidence.claim: System testing found no unresolved bugs affecting safety and efficiency.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary: System testing verifies that all functions configured in each module are adequate. No unresolved bugs have been found in this software that affect safety and efficiency.

evidence.claim: Risk acceptability classifies risk levels 1 through 4 as acceptable and 5 through 25 as unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable – Light Gray Zone (Risk Level 1 ~ 4) In this zone, risk is acceptable. Unacceptable – Dark Gray Zone (Risk Level 5 ~ 25) Elimination or mitigation actions must be taken at first to reduce the risk.

evidence.claim: Hazard analysis includes cybersecurity risks for login and data encryption, with residual acceptability listed as acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: HA-0009 Login Unauthorized access by unauthorized personnel Cybersecurity 3 2 Unacceptable Restrict random access by setting id / pw when connecting to Wireless network 2 2 Acceptable; HA-0010 Data encryption Unauthorized user can access the unencrypted data Cybersecurity 3 2 Unacceptable Protect the transmission data by applying encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, WPAPSK+TKIP, etc. 2 2 Acceptable

evidence.claim: The attachment list includes Requirements for Cybersecurity of Medical Devices.
evidence.evidenceText: 12. List of Attachment No. Content 1 Requirements for Cybersecurity of Medical Devices

evidence.claim: Revision history lists AXD software version 1.0.0 and AXD_Firmware version 1.0.0 with initial release date Jan/1/2025.
evidence.evidenceText: 1) Software version info Revision Date Name Description 1.0.0 Jan/1/2025 AXD Initial release 2) Firmware version info Revision Date Name Description 1.0.0 Jan/1/2025 AXD_Firmware Initial release

evidence.claim: The unresolved anomalies section lists none.
evidence.evidenceText: 14.2. Unresolved Anomalies No Problem Description Criticality Reason for Problem Potential Impact None

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal metadata says MFDS/domestic, but the source text references FDA guidance and 21 CFR 820.30; MFDS is not explicitly present in the source text.
Document revision date is inconsistent: most headers state Rev. Date Nov/23/2024, while pages 66-68 state Rev. Date Nov/15/2024.
Software and firmware revision history lists Jan/1/2025 initial releases, which is later than the stated document revision dates.
Some traceability and hazard identifiers appear inconsistent in OCR/source text, including HA-005 versus HA-0005 and SRC-C-2 versus SRS-C-2.
